text: BM25 works well when a query includes rare, discriminative terms from the local corpus. Search agents often waste calls when the first query is too broad.
Query expansion can add aliases such as lexical search, keyword ranking, retrieval query, and document lookup. These terms improve recall without requiring repeated rewrites.
Vector search and embedding retrieval are useful for semantic similarity, but they can miss exact identifiers, uncommon terminology, and negative constraints.
Agent orchestration benefits from focused subqueries, boosted terms, exclusions, and rate-limit aware retrieval plans that reduce latency and context churn.
